description: Flesh out stubbed/incomplete features in the content platform. Security stories (WS1-WS5) are done. This PRD covers the feature layer: AI client wiring, platform adapters, queue workers, analytics sync, asset regeneration, and Studio integration.

userStories.title: Wire AI client to @org/ai-core package
userStories.description: Replace the stub in apps/content-os/src/lib/ai.ts with a real implementation that delegates to @org/ai-core's createAIClient().
acceptanceCriteria: createContentOSAIClient() returns a working AIClient that delegates to @org/ai-core createAIClient()
AIChatParams maps correctly to ai-core ChatParams (userId, messages, model, maxTokens, temperature)
AIChatResult maps correctly from ai-core ChatResult (content, usage with tokensIn/tokensOut/costUsd, model, latencyMs)
aiClient singleton is initialized with appId 'content-os' and connects to Supabase for billing
TypeScript compiles with no errors (npm run typecheck passes)

userStories.title: Implement Bluesky platform adapter
userStories.description: Create apps/content-os/src/infrastructure/distribution/platforms/bluesky.adapter.ts implementing PlatformAdapter interface. Use AT Protocol for posting. Bluesky uses app passwords stored in PlatformCredentials.accessToken (identifier in metadata.handle). getAuthUrl and exchangeCode should throw 'Bluesky uses app passwords, not OAuth' error. createPost: create session with com.atproto.server.createSession, then com.atproto.repo.createRecord for app.bsky.feed.post. deletePost: com.atproto.repo.deleteRecord. getMetrics: return zeroed PlatformMetrics (Bluesky has no public metrics API). validateCredentials: attempt createSession and check success. Register in platforms/index.ts. Follow the same class structure as linkedin.adapter.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: BlueskyAdapter class implements PlatformAdapter interface with platform='bluesky'
createPost creates an AT Protocol record with text content via fetch to bsky.social PDS
deletePost removes the record via AT Protocol deleteRecord
getMetrics returns zeroed PlatformMetrics with a comment about no public API
validateCredentials verifies app password by calling createSession
Adapter exported from platforms/index.ts and registered via registerAdapter()
TypeScript compiles with no errors

userStories.title: Implement Threads platform adapter
userStories.description: Create apps/content-os/src/infrastructure/distribution/platforms/threads.adapter.ts implementing PlatformAdapter. Threads uses Meta's Threads API (graph.threads.net). OAuth via Meta. getAuthUrl: build URL to threads.net/oauth/authorize with threads_basic and threads_content_publish scopes. exchangeCode: POST to graph.threads.net/oauth/access_token. createPost: two-step — POST media container to {userId}/threads, then POST to {userId}/threads_publish. deletePost: DELETE to graph.threads.net/{postId}. getMetrics: GET {postId}/insights for views,likes,replies. validateCredentials: GET /me to check token validity. Register in platforms/index.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: ThreadsAdapter class implements PlatformAdapter with platform='threads'
OAuth flow generates correct Threads authorization URL with proper scopes
createPost uses two-step container+publish flow
getMetrics fetches insights (views, likes, replies, quotes)
Adapter exported from platforms/index.ts and registered
TypeScript compiles with no errors

userStories.title: Implement Reddit platform adapter
userStories.description: Create apps/content-os/src/infrastructure/distribution/platforms/reddit.adapter.ts implementing PlatformAdapter. Reddit OAuth2 via reddit.com. getAuthUrl: build URL to reddit.com/api/v1/authorize with identity,submit,read scopes, duration=permanent. exchangeCode: POST to reddit.com/api/v1/access_token with basic auth (clientId:clientSecret). createPost: POST to oauth.reddit.com/api/submit (kind=self, sr from metadata.subreddit, title from first line of text, text from rest). deletePost: POST to oauth.reddit.com/api/del. getMetrics: GET oauth.reddit.com/api/info?id=t3_{id} for score,ups,num_comments. validateCredentials: GET /api/v1/me. Register in platforms/index.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: RedditAdapter class implements PlatformAdapter with platform='reddit'
OAuth flow generates correct Reddit authorization URL with submit and read scopes
createPost submits a self-post to subreddit specified in metadata
getMetrics fetches post score, upvotes, num_comments
Adapter exported from platforms/index.ts and registered
TypeScript compiles with no errors

userStories.title: Implement analytics batch sync loop
userStories.description: Replace the TODO in apps/content-os/src/app/api/analytics/sync/route.ts. When no jobId provided: 1) Query distribution_jobs where status='published' AND (last_synced_at IS NULL OR last_synced_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '24 hours'). 2) For each job, call distributionService.fetchMetrics(job.id) wrapped in try/catch so one failure doesn't abort batch. 3) After fetching, update last_synced_at on the job. 4) Return { synced: N, errors: M, total: T }. Also register METRICS_SYNC queue worker in workers.ts that handles MetricsSyncJob by calling the same fetchMetrics logic for a single job. Add pg-boss cron schedule: boss.schedule('metrics-sync-cron', '0 */6 * * *', {}) to run every 6 hours.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/analytics/sync without jobId iterates all published jobs needing sync
Jobs synced within last 24 hours are skipped
Response includes { synced, errors, total } counts
Error in one job does not abort the batch
METRICS_SYNC worker registered in registerWorkers()
TypeScript compiles with no errors

userStories.title: Fix asset regeneration with context loading
userStories.description: Fix the stub in apps/content-os/src/services/asset-generator.service.ts regenerate() method. The service needs a reference to the derived-asset repo. Add it as a constructor dependency (alongside the AI client). In regenerate(): 1) Load existing asset by assetId from derivedAssetRepo.findById(). 2) Build a prompt that includes original body, assetType, platformHint, and the user's feedback. 3) Call this.ai.chat() with the enriched prompt. 4) Return the new asset preserving contentNodeId, assetType, platformHint, sourceSegmentIds from original, incrementing version. Update the service container (services/container.ts) to pass the repo when constructing AssetGeneratorService.
acceptanceCriteria: regenerate() loads existing asset by assetId from derived-asset repo
Prompt includes original body, asset type, platform hint, and user feedback
Returned asset preserves contentNodeId, assetType, sourceSegmentIds from original
Version number incremented from original asset version
Service container updated to pass derivedAssetRepo dependency
TypeScript compiles with no errors

userStories.title: Register clip-extract queue worker
userStories.description: Add CLIP_EXTRACT worker in apps/content-os/src/infrastructure/queue/workers.ts. Handler receives ClipExtractJob {nodeId, segmentId, sourceUrl, startMs, endMs}. Steps: 1) Download source video to temp file. 2) Use fluent-ffmpeg to extract clip from startMs to endMs (output to temp). 3) Upload extracted clip to Supabase storage bucket 'clips' with path clips/{nodeId}/{segmentId}.mp4. 4) Create or update derived_asset record with mediaUrl = storage URL, assetType='clip', status='draft'. 5) Clean up temp files. Handle errors by logging and rethrowing for pg-boss retry.
acceptanceCriteria: CLIP_EXTRACT worker registered in registerWorkers()
Worker extracts video clip using ffmpeg with startMs/endMs
Extracted clip uploaded to Supabase storage
Derived asset record created with mediaUrl
Temp files cleaned up in finally block
TypeScript compiles with no errors

userStories.title: Register distribution-publish queue worker
userStories.description: Add DISTRIBUTION_PUBLISH worker in workers.ts. Handler receives DistributionPublishJob {jobId, assetId, accountId}. Steps: 1) Load distribution_job by jobId, update status to 'publishing'. 2) Load derived_asset by assetId for body text and mediaUrls. 3) Load distribution_account by accountId for credentials and platform. 4) Decrypt credentials using token-encryption. 5) Get platform adapter via getAdapter(account.platform). 6) Call adapter.createPost() with credentials and asset content. 7) On success: update job status='published', set externalPostId and externalUrl. 8) On failure: update job status='failed', set errorMessage.
acceptanceCriteria: DISTRIBUTION_PUBLISH worker registered in registerWorkers()
Worker loads asset, account, and decrypts credentials
Worker resolves correct platform adapter
Successful publish updates job to 'published' with external IDs
Failed publish updates job to 'failed' with error message
TypeScript compiles with no errors

userStories.title: Fix queue UI null safety and date parsing
userStories.description: In apps/content-os/src/app/queue/page.tsx: 1) Add optional chaining on slot.asset?.body and slot.asset?.title where status==='filled'. 2) Wrap new Date(slot.scheduledFor) in a safeFormatDate helper that catches invalid dates and returns 'Invalid date'. 3) Wrap the Promise.all fetch in try/catch and add an error state with retry button. 4) Add loading skeleton while fetching.
acceptanceCriteria: SlotCard safely handles null/undefined asset when status is 'filled'
Malformed scheduledFor dates render fallback text instead of crashing
Failed API fetches show error state with retry button
TypeScript compiles with no errors

userStories.title: Add Studio compilation export to Content OS
userStories.description: Create apps/studio/src/app/api/compilations/[id]/export/route.ts. The endpoint: 1) Loads the compilation and its clips from Studio DB. 2) Creates a content_node in Content OS (type='video', title=compilation.title, sourceUrl=compilation.outputUrl). This can be a direct Supabase insert since both apps share the same instance. 3) For each clip, creates a derived_asset (type='clip', mediaUrl=clip.outputUrl, sourceSegmentIds=[]). 4) Returns { contentNodeId, assetCount }. Also add an 'Export to Content OS' button in the compilation detail UI that calls this endpoint.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/compilations/[id]/export endpoint exists in Studio
Endpoint creates content_node record in shared Supabase
Each clip becomes a derived_asset linked to the content_node
Response includes contentNodeId and asset count
TypeScript compiles with no errors
